texts.full_text: War II serving in the Mumansk Run in the Northern part of the Atlantic Ocean.After the war, he took a job as a purser for the United States Lines shipping company; notably serving on the SS United States when it captured the trans-Atlantic speed record on 4 July 1952. In 1948 he married Myra Warren whom he later divorced. While working for the United States Line, Sliker pursued university studies; first at Rutgers University and then Harvard University where he graduated Phi Beta Kappa with a bachelor's degree in languages in 1950. He concurrently studied music at the New England Conservatory where he graduated with a bachelor's degree in vocal performanceIt seems like you've provided some information about John Sliker now, should you likemeto extract any relevant details or pro

texts.full_text: out of memory. These figures clearly indicate that the variance reduced stochastic ADMM algorithms (including SAG-ADMM, SCAS-ADMM, SVRG-ADMM and ASVRG-ADMM) converge much faster than those without variance reduction techniques, e.g. STOC-ADMM and OPG-ADMM. Notably, ASVRG-ADMM consistently outperforms all other algorithms in terms of the convergence rate under all settings, which empirically verifies our theoretical result that ASVRG-ADMM has a faster convergence rate of $\mathcal{O}(1/T^{2})$, as opposed to the best known rate of $\mathcal{O}(1/T)$. ![Comparison of different stochastic ADMM methods fOr graph-guided logistic regression problems on the two data sets: a9a (top) and w8a (bottom).[]{data-label="fig_sim2"}](fig31 "fig:"){width="0.486\columnwidth"} ![Comparison of different stochastic ADMM methods for graph-guided logistic regression problems on the two data sets: a9a (top) and w8a (bottom).[]{data-label="fig_sim2"}](fig32 "fig:"){width="0.486\columnwidth"} ![Comparison of different stochastic ADMM methods for graph-guided logistic regression
